title: Runners Help Tired Man Finish Marathon
summary: At the Boston Marathon, a runner named Ajay Haridasse fell down near the finish line and couldn't get up. He was very tired and tried to stand but kept falling. Many runners passed by, but two kind runners, Aaron Beggs and Robson De Oliveira, stopped to help him. They put their arms around Ajay and helped him walk to the finish line. They did this even though it made their own race times slower. People watching cheered and filmed the nice moment. Ajay thanked the runners for helping him when he needed it most. This story shows how important it is to help others.

background_read: A marathon is a long-distance running event that is 26.2 miles (about 42 kilometers) long. People train for months to run marathons, and it can be very tiring. The Boston Marathon is one of the most famous marathons in the world, held every year in Boston, USA. Runners from many countries participate, and it's a big event with lots of spectators. Sometimes, runners get exhausted and need help, and this story shows how kindness can make a big difference during a race.
Article_Structure: WHO: Aaron Beggs and Robson De Oliveira, two runners at the Boston Marathon
WHAT: They helped an exhausted runner, Ajay Haridasse, who fell and couldn't get up near the finish line
WHERE: At the Boston Marathon, near the finish line
WHY: To show kindness and help someone in need, even though it slowed down their own race times

perspectives.description: The story highlights the importance of stopping to help people who are struggling, even if it means giving up personal goals.
